threads.label: Ukraine — 40-Day Operation
threads.status_line: Zelenskyy declared 40-day SBU influence operation 25 Jun to compel Russia to end war; 660-drone attack 26 Jun (one of largest ever) targeting 12 Russian regions and energy infrastructure; Crimea state of emergency; Stanovaya: Putin compelled not dominant — escalation risk elevated
expand_if: Russia executes major coordinated civilian infrastructure strike in retaliation
NATO Article 4 consultation invoked
Zelensky proposes ceasefire terms or 40-day operation produces diplomatic back-channel
Russia conducts strike killing more than 50 civilians in single attack
Belarus officially drawn into conflict

threads.label: Iran / Hormuz
threads.status_line: Roadmap agreed 22 Jun; IAEA invited back; 60-day sanctions waiver on oil; Brent $72 (-10% wk on Hormuz risk unwind); inspection dispute over bombed Fordow/Natanz/Isfahan sites — Iran denies US claims of agreement; Lebanon MOU still unreleased; 440kg enriched uranium at 60% unverified
expand_if: IAEA inspection modalities publicly announced (or collapsed)
Lebanon MOU provisions published
Brent breaks above $90 on deal-collapse signals
Iran publicly disputes or pauses the 60-day clock
Iranian enrichment activity confirmed resumed at bombed sites
Israeli action in Lebanon triggers deal collapse

threads.label: UK Labour Leadership
threads.status_line: Starmer resigned 22 Jun; Burnham returned to Parliament same day; contest opens 9 Jul; Burnham running, no centrist declared; gilts muted (10Y ~4.85%, 30Y ~5.50%); sterling $1.32 — political resolution not fresh uncertainty
expand_if: Centrist candidate publicly declares before 9 Jul
Burnham makes fiscal commitment deviating from current rules
Major gilt or sterling move on leadership development
Cabinet minister publicly backs or opposes Burnham
Result of contest announced

threads.label: Alliance of Sahel States
threads.status_line: No follow-up attack post 18 Jun Niamey airport; JNIM-ISSP competitive dynamic documented; ACLED: departure from rural insurgency to coordinated infrastructure strikes; AES security premise under test but no new incident
expand_if: JNIM or ISSP follow-up attack on AES capital or infrastructure
Alliance of Sahel States announces new bilateral agreements (Russia, China)
ECOWAS or AU imposes sanctions or condemnation
Major attack on capital city (Bamako, Niamey, Ouagadougou)
Wagner/Africa Corps publicly expands presence
Jihadist corridor reaches coastal West Africa

threads.label: South Africa Xenophobia Crisis
threads.status_line: Jun 30 deadline today; military deployed to airports and strategic sites; police pre-positioned; 2+ dead in preceding weeks; Nigeria/Ghana/Mozambique/Malawi/Zimbabwe repatriation flights; trade -22%; Ramaphosa co-opting deportations not confronting movement legally
expand_if: Mass violence on or after 30 Jun in major cities
AU formal condemnation or UNHCR emergency declaration
Cross-border trade contraction exceeds 30%
Violence spreads to Johannesburg or Cape Town at scale
Country recalls ambassador from South Africa
South African government criminalises or formally endorses movement

threads.label: World Cup 2026
threads.status_line: England 2-0 Panama 27 Jun Boston; Bellingham scored, Kane all-time England WC top scorer; top Group L; R32 vs DR Congo Wednesday Atlanta 5pm UK; nostalgia chart wave running alongside campaign
expand_if: England vs DR Congo R32 result
Major injury to Kane or Bellingham
Cultural flashpoint around tournament
England reach quarter-finals

threads.label: Israel / Lebanon
threads.status_line: Jun 27 analysis: ceasefire framework now explicitly tied to Hezbollah disarmament; comprehensive talks ongoing week of Jun 22; Lebanon MOU provisions still unreleased; Israel-Hezbollah exchange of fire continues at low level
expand_if: Lebanon MOU provisions revealed
Major Israeli ground incursion or Hezbollah rocket barrage
Hezbollah formally accepts or rejects disarmament framework
Iran cash injection to Hezbollah confirmed publicly
Hezbollah-Israel full ceasefire formally negotiated

threads.label: Ebola Bundibugyo — DRC / France
threads.status_line: 1,155 confirmed DRC (from 837 Jun 21); 304 deaths (from 196); France case confirmed 24 Jun = first European case; Uganda clear (no new cases since 5 Jun); no approved Bundibugyo vaccine; WHO rates response 3-4/10; Ituri 1,054 of cases
expand_if: Additional cases confirmed in Europe or other high-income countries
WHO raises PHEIC to highest level
Vaccine supply or pipeline announcement
Confirmed community transmission outside DRC
Daily rate confirmed above 50 new cases/day sustained

threads.label: UK Economic Stress
threads.status_line: Brent $72 (-10% wk on Hormuz unwind); FTSE ~10,508; 30Y gilt ~5.50%; sterling $1.32; 10Y gilt ~4.85%; Starmer resignation produced muted market move — gilt-political gap holds
expand_if: Gilt yield spike above 5.8% with upward trajectory
Sterling drops materially on Labour leadership chaos
Energy price cap revision announced
Bank of England emergency action
Brent above $90 on Iran deal collapse
Burnham fiscal commitment deviating from current rules moves markets

threads.label: SpaceX IPO / Musk
threads.status_line: SPCX hit $147.11 all-time low 23 Jun (below $180 floor); recovered to ~$153 by week-end; Nasdaq -4.6% wk on chip sell-off; Musk briefly trillionaire on IPO momentum before pullback; no political/regulatory development
expand_if: SPCX breaks below $147 all-time low
Musk officially and sustained above trillion-dollar net worth
Starlink or xAI regulatory decision affected by political relationship
Musk makes significant public statement deviating from Trump administration line

threads.label: Colombia — De la Espriella Presidency
threads.status_line: Won runoff 49.66% vs 48.70% (much tighter than 8-pt poll lead); far-right, Trump-backed; confirmed 24 Jun; to restore Israel ties, end FARC talks, boost oil/gas, reduce state 40%; first far-right Colombian president; no prior political experience
expand_if: Cabinet appointments signal specific policy direction
Formal resumption or termination of FARC peace talks
Israel relations formally restored or embassy move announced
Colombia-Venezuela military cooperation on Tren de Aragua
Economic response to inherited debt headwinds
US-Colombia formal security cooperation agreement signed

retired_threads.retired_because: Time-bounded event: election day Jun 21. Superseded by colombia-espriella thread opened Jun 28.
retired_threads.revive_if: Electoral dispute or fraud allegation. Superseded.

retired_threads.revive_if: France announces alternative bilateral fighter partnership; Germany announces F-35 or US-alternative procurement at scale; EU defence ministers joint communiqué on post-FCAS strategy; UK invited into new Franco-German defence framework

retired_threads.revive_if: Mungiu or another winner makes a politically significant statement in the press circuit; or Fjord triggers a specific diplomatic controversy

retired_threads.retired_because: 7 consecutive days stable with last_changed 2026-05-22; chart positions unchanged — divergence already documented

retired_threads.revive_if: New major episode on Iran deal, Russia, or Ukraine; public clash with Trump administration escalates; platform change

retired_threads.label: SNP Crisis / Murrell
retired_threads.retired_because: Sentencing delivered 23 Jun — 5 years 3 months for embezzling £400k from SNP. Revive condition was met (significant prison term). Event is now concluded; no new allegations emerged; Sturgeon confirmed no action against her. Story absorbed into background political noise.
retired_threads.revive_if: Sturgeon makes significant public statement; appeal launched; new SNP leadership challenge citing Murrell case; Operation Branchform produces further charges

retired_threads.retired_because: 8 consecutive stable days since last_changed 2026-06-01; Imamoglu corruption trial and espionage trial both ongoing but no verdict, release, or major new development
retired_threads.revive_if: Imamoglu released or verdict delivered; CHP headquarters sealed permanently; street protests escalate to general strike; EU or US formal condemnation

retired_threads.retired_because: 8 consecutive stable days since last_changed 2026-06-01; lean season started June as projected; no new IPC famine declaration, funding announcement, or ceasefire
retired_threads.revive_if: IPC declares additional famine zones; major donor announces significant new funding; ceasefire between SAF and RSF; humanitarian corridor agreement

retired_threads.revive_if: Parliament convenes; major walkout by minority representatives; constitutional amendment proposed; Kurdish/minority representation disputes escalate

retired_threads.retired_because: 8 consecutive stable days since last_changed 2026-06-09; no incident since Jun 5 Constanta drone; NATO drone protocols still in discussion with no resolution

retired_threads.label: Mearsheimer
retired_threads.retired_because: 8 consecutive stable days since last_changed 2026-06-09; Iran deal emerged without Netanyahu torpedoing it, partially complicating the Netanyahu-veto thesis; no new post or appearance
retired_threads.revive_if: New post or appearance shifting the argument; Netanyahu publicly opposes Iran MOU; Trump-Netanyahu public confrontation; deal collapses and Netanyahu's opposition is cited

retired_threads.label: Belfast Riots / UK Anti-Immigrant Violence
retired_threads.retired_because: Riots subsided; story fully absorbed into the Labour leadership contest. Burnham won Makerfield Jun 19; political fallout tracked under uk-labour-leadership. PSNI confirmed no paramilitary coordination.
retired_threads.revive_if: Further severe nights of unrest in Northern Ireland or English cities; PSNI finds paramilitary coordination evidence; violence explicitly cited as factor in Labour leadership campaign

source_discoveries.name: Richard Haass — 'Home & Away' Substack
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Former CFR president arguing Trump is recreating the JCPOA he destroyed, and that regime change pressure produced a more hardline Iranian government — structural counter-frame to deal optimism

source_discoveries.name: L'Orient Today
source_discoveries.why_interesting: English-language edition of L'Orient Le Jour; takes a meaningfully different political line from Al-Nahar on Lebanese politics; useful for south Lebanon coverage

source_discoveries.name: Asharq Al-Awsat (English)
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Gulf-backed pan-Arab daily; broke the Syria parliament June 8 story before other outlets; consistently ahead on Gulf/Arab political transitions

source_discoveries.name: Velina Tchakarova — Substack
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Vienna-based geopolitical analyst; Austrian/Central European perspective on Ukraine distinct from Washington/London axis

source_discoveries.name: Alastair Crooke — Conflicts Forum Substack
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Former senior British diplomat and MI6 officer; argues Hezbollah advises Iran rather than takes orders; 'America's Suez Crisis' thesis; Jun 2026 prediction confirmed within 24h on Jun 11

source_discoveries.name: Trita Parsi — Quincy Institute / Responsible Statecraft
source_discoveries.why_interesting: NIAC founder, Quincy Institute fellow; consistent frame that Trump is not in a dominant position; Jun 12 'Warmongers in meltdown' piece names the Washington institutional battle most clearly

source_discoveries.name: Foundation for Defense of Democracies (FDD)
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Hawkish Washington think tank; building legal/institutional case for rejecting any Iran deal as insufficient; tracks closely with Netanyahu's likely response

source_discoveries.name: Hamidreza Azizi — SWP Berlin
source_discoveries.why_interesting: German-Iranian scholar at SWP; insider read on Iranian domestic discourse; cited by Fortune June 13 on birthday timing factor in deal delay

source_discoveries.name: Just Security
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Only serious legal/ethical analysis of the Khamenei assassination's precedent implications; argues deal's signing retroactively validates assassination as diplomatic strategy

source_discoveries.name: Carnegie Endowment — Emissary
source_discoveries.why_interesting: May 2026 piece argues Iran may quietly rebuild nuclear programme once Hormuz leverage is established — the deal defers rather than resolves the nuclear question

source_discoveries.name: Pan African Visions
source_discoveries.why_interesting: June 2026 structural analysis of South Africa xenophobia connecting the crisis to AfCFTA failure and ANC governance collapse; African-perspective framing entirely absent from Western coverage

source_discoveries.name: The Mekong Memo — August Whitcraft
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Substack covering Laos/Mekong region with granular depth; surfaced the Russia-Laos nuclear cooperation + OSJD rail corridor story — the parallel architecture Russia is building while Western attention is on Geneva

source_discoveries.name: Russia's Pivot to Asia
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Tracks Russia's systematic Rosatom nuclear client-building and infrastructure lock-in across the Global South; documents the pattern that the Iran deal does not reverse

source_discoveries.name: Stimson Center — Sahel Program
source_discoveries.why_interesting: June 2026 briefing on Alliance of Sahel States military escalation and jihadist sophistication; one of the only serious English-language trackers of the confederation's development

source_discoveries.name: ACLED Conflict Sahel
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Real-time conflict event data for Mali/Niger/Burkina; documents JNIM and ISSP tactical evolution; expert comment on Niamey airport attack is the best structural analysis of the Jun 18 attack

source_discoveries.name: Narges Bajoghli — Johns Hopkins SAIS
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Argues US analysis systematically misreads Iranian domestic politics by conflating regime weakness with popular opposition; her framing — that the MOU represents Iran's version of a victory — is almost entirely absent from Western coverage of the deal

source_discoveries.name: Tatiana Stanovaya — R.Politik / Riddle Russia
source_discoveries.why_interesting: Moscow-based political analyst; argues Russia's military advantage is dissipating and Putin has almost no escalatory tools left that don't risk catastrophic blowback — 'escalation is the only way to respond to a situation you can't control'; counter-frame to both Western triumphalism and Kremlin maximalism on Ukraine's 40-day operation
